title: Chef and Average on a Tree

content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tree with **N** nodes. Each node of the tree has an integer weight associated with it.

Let's define the cost of a sequence of numbers as the arithmetic mean of all elements of the sequence.

Next, let's define the cost of a path in the tree as the cost of the sequence of weights of all nodes belonging to the path. (It's possible for a path to contain only one node.)

A set of paths in the tree is called a correct path decomposition if each node of the tree belongs to exactly one of the paths from this set. The cost of a correct decomposition is defined as the minimum of costs of all paths in this decomposition.

Chef would like to find the maximum cost of a correct decomposition. Can you help him?
constraints: 1 ≤ sum of N over all test cases ≤ 200,000
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN denoting the weights of nodes of the tree. Each of the following N-1 lines contains two space-separated integers x and y denoting an edge between nodes x and y.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum possible cost among all correct decompositions. The answer will be considered correct if its absolute error does not exceed 10^-6.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the maximum cost of a correct decomposition is (2 + 3) / 2 = 2.5. For the second test case, the maximum cost of a correct decomposition is (5 + 2 + 1) / 3 = 2.666667.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a dynamic programming approach. We will calculate the maximum cost of a correct decomposition for each subtree rooted at each node. We will consider all possible ways to decompose the subtree into paths and calculate the cost of each decomposition. We will then choose the decomposition with the maximum cost.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) — The time complexity is O(N^2) because we are iterating over all nodes and for each node, we are iterating over all its children.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — The space complexity is O(N) because we are storing the maximum cost of a correct decomposition for each node.
